summary: Formatting-only cleanup that cherry-picked make-format related work from PR #636 and ran `make format` across a wide slice of the tree. The main mechanical effect was clang-format style normalization, especially moving opening braces for multi-line `if`/`for` conditions onto a new line, plus adding missing trailing newlines in some files. It touched Dolphin library code, fighter code, stage/ground files, item files, and baselib files without evidence of semantic decomp changes.
what_changed: Ran `make format` after cherry-picking formatting-related commits from PR #636, according to the PR body excerpt.
Updated 62 files with 215 additions and 126 deletions, primarily whitespace and brace placement churn.

Changed many multi-line conditional statements so the closing parenthesis ends the condition line and the opening brace is placed on the next line, e.g. `if (... )\n{` in CARDCheck.c, GXFifo.c, fighter.c, and many fighter character files.
Added or restored a trailing newline at the end of `src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.c`, replacing the previous `No newline at end of file` state.
Touched both source and header files, including fighter, ground/stage, item, Dolphin CARD/OS/GX, and sysdolphin baselib files.
Review context indicates `.clang-format` was also involved, with a reviewer noting that a trailing newline may have been accidentally removed there.
smart_moves: Separated formatting churn into its own PR, reducing noise for functional or matching PRs and making later diffs easier to review.
Used the project formatting target (`make format`) rather than hand-formatting individual call sites, keeping style changes consistent across many systems.
Applied formatting broadly to existing decomp code paths where clang-format had deterministic output, helping stabilize future formatter runs.
The visible diffs avoid symbol renames or code restructuring; this keeps the PR suitable as a mechanical baseline cleanup.
decomp_lessons: Keep formatter-only changes isolated from matching or semantic decomp work; otherwise brace/whitespace churn can obscure meaningful codegen tactics.
For this repository's style at the time, long multi-line `if` and `for` conditions may format with the opening brace on its own line after the condition closes.
Trailing newline changes are part of formatter hygiene but should be checked carefully, including in config files such as `.clang-format`.
Large formatting passes can span unrelated systems; reviewers and future agents should treat these as baseline style normalization unless the diff shows actual expression or declaration changes.
When using `make format`, expect small one-line hunks across many fighter, ground, item, Dolphin, and baselib files due to line wrapping and brace placement.
naming_conventions: No new naming convention was introduced by this PR.

Existing project names were preserved, including fighter action-state names such as `AS_GAMEWATCH_ATTACK11`, `AS_NESS_SPECIALN_HOLD`, struct/member style such as `x10_action_state_index`, and Dolphin-style names such as `CARD_RESULT_BUSY` and `__OSUnlockSramEx`.
The PR demonstrates that formatting cleanup should not rename unknown symbols or inferred fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching tactic is evidenced beyond formatting hygiene.

The only assembly-adjacent visible change is adding a trailing newline after the closing brace of `asm void PPCMtwpar()` in `src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.c`; there is no evidence of instruction or matching logic changes.
For matching PRs, reuse the tactic of keeping formatter churn separate so codegen-sensitive edits can be reviewed without unrelated whitespace noise.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.lesson: Even formatter/config edits should preserve final newlines; check config files after running automated formatting.

searchable_terms: No newline at end of file
follow_up_queries: Find PR #636 formatting-related commits that were cherry-picked into PR #637.
Check whether `.clang-format` ended PR #637 with a final newline after reviewer feedback.
Search later PRs for formatter churn caused by the same multi-line condition brace rule.
Identify whether any files touched by PR #637 later required semantic fixes due to formatting-only assumptions.
